question: Will the price of Ethereum be above $2,100 on April 10?

fragility_points: A sharp overnight or morning crypto selloff could erase the cushion.
Single-minute settlement makes path volatility more important than end-of-day regime.
key_assumptions: ETH stays in roughly the current price regime through the settlement minute.
The relevant settlement minute is 12:00 ET = 16:00 UTC on Binance.
Binance API kline close aligns with the chart-based settlement surface.
main_logical_chain: Check the contract and verify the governing source of truth is Binance ETH/USDT 1-minute close at 12:00 ET.
Verify Binance uses UTC server time and map 12:00 ET on April 10 to 16:00 UTC.
Observe ETH spot is already materially above 2100.
Use recent Binance history as a base-rate anchor for next-day persistence when ETH is already above the threshold.
Discount the anchor somewhat because this contract resolves on a single minute rather than a daily close.
payload.main_thesis: Yes is the right side because ETH is already comfortably above 2100 and short-horizon persistence is usually strong, but the market likely overstates certainty for a one-minute timestamped threshold.
quote_anchors: 67/73 recent prior-above-2100 daily cases also closed above 2100 the next day
payload.source_quality_view: Primary source quality is high because Binance is the designated settlement source; contextual evidence quality is decent but not independent because it also relies on Binance data.
strongest_disconfirmers: Resolution depends on one exact 1-minute close, so ordinary intraday volatility can still produce a No even if the broader regime stays healthy.
strongest_supports: Binance spot price during the check was about 2213, roughly $113 above the threshold.
In the recent 120-day Binance sample, next-day close stayed above 2100 in 67 of 73 cases when the prior close was already above 2100.
Settlement is less than a day away, favoring persistence absent a shock.
payload.timing_relevance: Very high: the market resolves on one exact noon ET minute on April 10, so short-horizon volatility and timestamp interpretation matter materially.
unresolved_ambiguities: The rules reference Binance chart candles while the audit used Binance API endpoints, though these should economically align.
The exact UI labeling of the noon candle is not directly visible from text-only fetch.
payload.what_would_change_view: A move of ETH down toward or below 2100 before noon ET, or verified evidence that the settlement timestamp/candle interpretation differs from 16:00 UTC, would lower confidence materially.
